fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the run.

fields.name: data_source
fields.description: Details about the run's data source.

types.name: CreateEvalRunRequestDataSource
types.description: Details about the run's data source.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of data source. Always `jsonl`.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Determines what populates the `item` namespace in the data source.

types.description: A JsonlRunDataSource object with that specifies a JSONL file that matches the eval

types.name: CreateEvalJsonlRunDataSourceSource
types.description: Determines what populates the `item` namespace in the data source.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of jsonl source. Always `file_content`.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The content of the jsonl file.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of jsonl source. Always `file_id`.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The identifier of the file.

types.name: CreateEvalCompletionsRunDataSource

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of run data source. Always `completions`.

fields.name: input_messages
fields.description: Used when sampling from a model. Dictates the structure of the messages passed into the model. Can either be a reference to a prebuilt trajectory (ie, `item.input_trajectory`), or a template with variable references to the `item` namespace.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The name of the model to use for generating completions (e.g. "o3-mini").

fields.name: source
fields.description: Determines what populates the `item` namespace in this run's data source.

types.description: A CompletionsRunDataSource object describing a model sampling configuration.

types.name: CreateEvalCompletionsRunDataSourceInputMessages
types.description: Used when sampling from a model. Dictates the structure of the messages passed into the model. Can either be a reference to a prebuilt trajectory (ie, `item.input_trajectory`), or a template with variable references to the `item` namespace.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of input messages. Always `template`.

fields.name: template
fields.description: A list of chat messages forming the prompt or context. May include variable references to the `item` namespace, ie {{item.name}}.

types.name: EasyInputMessage

fields.name: role
fields.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

fields.name: content
fields.description:
Text, image, or audio input to the model, used to generate a response.
Can also contain previous assistant responses.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the message input. Always `message`.

types.description:
A message input to the model with a role indicating instruction following
hierarchy. Instructions given with the `developer` or `system` role take
precedence over instructions given with the `user` role. Messages with the
`assistant` role are presumed to have been generated by the model in previous
interactions.

types.name: EasyInputMessageRole
types.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

types.name: EasyInputMessageContent
types.description:
Text, image, or audio input to the model, used to generate a response.
Can also contain previous assistant responses.

types.name: InputTextContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_text`.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text input to the model.

types.description: A text input to the model.

types.name: InputImageContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_image`.

types.description: An image input to the model. Learn about [image inputs](/docs/guides/vision).

types.name: InputFileContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_file`.

fields.name: filename
fields.description: The name of the file to be sent to the model.

fields.name: file_data
fields.description: The content of the file to be sent to the model.

fields.name: file_url
fields.description: The URL of the file to be sent to the model.

types.description: A file input to the model.

types.name: MessagePhase
types.description:
Labels an `assistant` message as intermediate commentary (`commentary`) or the final answer (`final_answer`).
For models like `gpt-5.3-codex` and beyond, when sending follow-up requests, preserve and resend
phase on all assistant messages — dropping it can degrade performance. Not used for user messages.

types.name: EvalItem

fields.name: role
fields.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the message input. Always `message`.

types.description:
A message input to the model with a role indicating instruction following
hierarchy. Instructions given with the `developer` or `system` role take
precedence over instructions given with the `user` role. Messages with the
`assistant` role are presumed to have been generated by the model in previous
interactions.

types.name: EvalItemContent
types.description: Inputs to the model - can contain template strings. Supports text, output text, input images, and input audio, either as a single item or an array of items.

types.name: EvalItemContentItem
types.description: A single content item: input text, output text, input image, or input audio.

types.name: EvalItemContentOutputText

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the output text. Always `output_text`.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text output from the model.

types.description: A text output from the model.

types.name: EvalItemInputImage

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the image input. Always `input_image`.

fields.name: image_url
fields.description: The URL of the image input.

fields.name: detail
fields.description: The detail level of the image to be sent to the model. One of `high`, `low`, or `auto`. Defaults to `auto`.

types.description: An image input block used within EvalItem content arrays.

types.name: InputAudio

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_audio`.

types.description: An audio input to the model.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Base64-encoded audio data.

fields.name: format
fields.description:
The format of the audio data. Currently supported formats are `mp3` and
`wav`.

types.name: InputAudioInputAudioFormat
types.description:
The format of the audio data. Currently supported formats are `mp3` and
`wav`.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of input messages. Always `item_reference`.

fields.name: item_reference
fields.description: A reference to a variable in the `item` namespace. Ie, "item.input_trajectory"

fields.name: temperature
fields.description: A higher temperature increases randomness in the outputs.

fields.name: max_completion_tokens
fields.description: The maximum number of tokens in the generated output.

fields.name: top_p
fields.description: An alternative to temperature for nucleus sampling; 1.0 includes all tokens.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: A seed value to initialize the randomness, during sampling.

fields.name: response_format
fields.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Setting to `{ "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...} }` enables
Structured Outputs which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON
schema. Learn more in the [Structured Outputs
guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.

fields.name: tools
fields.description: A list of tools the model may call. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. Use this to provide a list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for. A max of 128 functions are supported.

types.name: ReasoningEffort
types.description:
Constrains effort on reasoning for
[reasoning models](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning).
Currently supported values are `none`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, and `xhigh`. Reducing
reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used
on reasoning in a response.

- `gpt-5.1` defaults to `none`, which does not perform reasoning. The supported reasoning values for `gpt-5.1` are `none`, `low`, `medium`, and `high`. Tool calls are supported for all reasoning values in gpt-5.1.
- All models before `gpt-5.1` default to `medium` reasoning effort, and do not support `none`.
- The `gpt-5-pro` model defaults to (and only supports) `high` reasoning effort.
- `xhigh` is supported for all models after `gpt-5.1-codex-max`.

types.name: CreateEvalCompletionsRunDataSourceSamplingParamsResponseFormat
types.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Setting to `{ "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...} }` enables
Structured Outputs which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON
schema. Learn more in the [Structured Outputs
guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.

types.name: ResponseFormatText

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `text`.

types.description: Default response format. Used to generate text responses.

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonSchema

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

fields.name: json_schema
fields.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

types.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonSchemaJsonSchema

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

types.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonObject

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_object`.

types.description:
JSON object response format. An older method of generating JSON responses.
Using `json_schema` is recommended for models that support it. Note that the
model will not generate JSON without a system or user message instructing it
to do so.

types.name: ChatCompletionTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

types.description: A function tool that can be used to generate a response.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

types.name: CreateEvalCompletionsRunDataSourceSource
types.description: Determines what populates the `item` namespace in this run's data source.

types.name: EvalStoredCompletionsSource

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of source. Always `stored_completions`.

types.description: A StoredCompletionsRunDataSource configuration describing a set of filters

types.name: CreateEvalResponsesRunDataSource

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of run data source. Always `responses`.

fields.name: input_messages
fields.description: Used when sampling from a model. Dictates the structure of the messages passed into the model. Can either be a reference to a prebuilt trajectory (ie, `item.input_trajectory`), or a template with variable references to the `item` namespace.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The name of the model to use for generating completions (e.g. "o3-mini").

fields.name: source
fields.description: Determines what populates the `item` namespace in this run's data source.

types.description: A ResponsesRunDataSource object describing a model sampling configuration.

types.name: CreateEvalResponsesRunDataSourceInputMessages
types.description: Used when sampling from a model. Dictates the structure of the messages passed into the model. Can either be a reference to a prebuilt trajectory (ie, `item.input_trajectory`), or a template with variable references to the `item` namespace.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of input messages. Always `template`.

fields.name: template
fields.description: A list of chat messages forming the prompt or context. May include variable references to the `item` namespace, ie {{item.name}}.

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the message (e.g. "system", "assistant", "user").

fields.name: content
fields.description: The content of the message.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of input messages. Always `item_reference`.

fields.name: item_reference
fields.description: A reference to a variable in the `item` namespace. Ie, "item.name"

fields.name: temperature
fields.description: A higher temperature increases randomness in the outputs.

fields.name: max_completion_tokens
fields.description: The maximum number of tokens in the generated output.

fields.name: top_p
fields.description: An alternative to temperature for nucleus sampling; 1.0 includes all tokens.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: A seed value to initialize the randomness, during sampling.

fields.name: tools
fields.description:
An array of tools the model may call while generating a response. You
can specify which tool to use by setting the `tool_choice` parameter.

The two categories of tools you can provide the model are:

- **Built-in tools**: Tools that are provided by OpenAI that extend the
  model's capabilities, like [web search](/docs/guides/tools-web-search)
  or [file search](/docs/guides/tools-file-search). Learn more about
  [built-in tools](/docs/guides/tools).
- **Function calls (custom tools)**: Functions that are defined by you,
  enabling the model to call your own code. Learn more about
  [function calling](/docs/guides/function-calling).

fields.name: text
fields.description:
Configuration options for a text response from the model. Can be plain
text or structured JSON data. Learn more:
- [Text inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/text)
- [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs)

types.name: Tool
types.description: A tool that can be used to generate a response.

types.name: FunctionTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the function tool. Always `function`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

fields.name: defer_loading
fields.description: Whether this function is deferred and loaded via tool search.

types.description: Defines a function in your own code the model can choose to call. Learn more about [function calling](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling).

types.name: FileSearchTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the file search tool. Always `file_search`.

fields.name: vector_store_ids
fields.description: The IDs of the vector stores to search.

fields.name: max_num_results
fields.description: The maximum number of results to return. This number should be between 1 and 50 inclusive.

types.description: A tool that searches for relevant content from uploaded files. Learn more about the [file search tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-file-search).

fields.name: score_threshold
fields.description: The score threshold for the file search, a number between 0 and 1. Numbers closer to 1 will attempt to return only the most relevant results, but may return fewer results.

fields.name: embedding_weight
fields.description: The weight of the embedding in the reciprocal ranking fusion.

fields.name: text_weight
fields.description: The weight of the text in the reciprocal ranking fusion.

types.name: ComparisonFilter

fields.name: type
fields.description:
Specifies the comparison operator: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `in`, `nin`.
- `eq`: equals
- `ne`: not equal
- `gt`: greater than
- `gte`: greater than or equal
- `lt`: less than
- `lte`: less than or equal
- `in`: in
- `nin`: not in

fields.name: key
fields.description: The key to compare against the value.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The value to compare against the attribute key; supports string, number, or boolean types.

types.description: A filter used to compare a specified attribute key to a given value using a defined comparison operation.

types.name: ComparisonFilterType
types.description:
Specifies the comparison operator: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `in`, `nin`.
- `eq`: equals
- `ne`: not equal
- `gt`: greater than
- `gte`: greater than or equal
- `lt`: less than
- `lte`: less than or equal
- `in`: in
- `nin`: not in

types.name: ComparisonFilterValue
types.description: The value to compare against the attribute key; supports string, number, or boolean types.

types.name: CompoundFilter

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of operation: `and` or `or`.

fields.name: filters
fields.description: Array of filters to combine. Items can be `ComparisonFilter` or `CompoundFilter`.

types.description: Combine multiple filters using `and` or `or`.

types.name: CompoundFilterType
types.description: Type of operation: `and` or `or`.

types.name: ComputerTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the computer tool. Always `computer`.

types.description: A tool that controls a virtual computer. Learn more about the [computer tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-computer-use).

types.name: ComputerUsePreviewTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the computer use tool. Always `computer_use_preview`.

fields.name: display_width
fields.description: The width of the computer display.

fields.name: display_height
fields.description: The height of the computer display.

types.description: A tool that controls a virtual computer. Learn more about the [computer tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-computer-use).

types.name: WebSearchTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the web search tool. One of `web_search` or `web_search_2025_08_26`.

fields.name: search_context_size
fields.description: High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the search. One of `low`, `medium`, or `high`. `medium` is the default.

types.description:
Search the Internet for sources related to the prompt. Learn more about the
[web search tool](/docs/guides/tools-web-search).

types.name: WebSearchToolType
types.description: The type of the web search tool. One of `web_search` or `web_search_2025_08_26`.

types.name: WebSearchToolFilters
types.description: Filters for the search.

types.name: WebSearchApproximateLocation

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of location approximation. Always `approximate`.

types.description: The approximate location of the user.

types.name: WebSearchToolSearchContextSize
types.description: High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the search. One of `low`, `medium`, or `high`. `medium` is the default.

types.name: McpTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the MCP tool. Always `mcp`.

fields.name: server_label
fields.description: A label for this MCP server, used to identify it in tool calls.

fields.name: server_url
fields.description:
The URL for the MCP server. One of `server_url` or `connector_id` must be
provided.

fields.name: connector_id
fields.description:
Identifier for service connectors, like those available in ChatGPT. One of
`server_url` or `connector_id` must be provided. Learn more about service
connectors [here](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp#connectors).

Currently supported `connector_id` values are:

- Dropbox: `connector_dropbox`
- Gmail: `connector_gmail`
- Google Calendar: `connector_googlecalendar`
- Google Drive: `connector_googledrive`
- Microsoft Teams: `connector_microsoftteams`
- Outlook Calendar: `connector_outlookcalendar`
- Outlook Email: `connector_outlookemail`
- SharePoint: `connector_sharepoint`

fields.name: authorization
fields.description:
An OAuth access token that can be used with a remote MCP server, either
with a custom MCP server URL or a service connector. Your application
must handle the OAuth authorization flow and provide the token here.

fields.name: server_description
fields.description: Optional description of the MCP server, used to provide more context.

fields.name: defer_loading
fields.description: Whether this MCP tool is deferred and discovered via tool search.

types.description:
Give the model access to additional tools via remote Model Context Protocol
(MCP) servers. [Learn more about MCP](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp).

types.name: McpToolConnectorId
types.description:
Identifier for service connectors, like those available in ChatGPT. One of
`server_url` or `connector_id` must be provided. Learn more about service
connectors [here](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp#connectors).

Currently supported `connector_id` values are:

- Dropbox: `connector_dropbox`
- Gmail: `connector_gmail`
- Google Calendar: `connector_googlecalendar`
- Google Drive: `connector_googledrive`
- Microsoft Teams: `connector_microsoftteams`
- Outlook Calendar: `connector_outlookcalendar`
- Outlook Email: `connector_outlookemail`
- SharePoint: `connector_sharepoint`

types.name: McpToolAllowedTools
types.description: List of allowed tool names or a filter object.

types.name: McpToolFilter

fields.name: tool_names
fields.description: List of allowed tool names.

fields.name: read_only
fields.description:
Indicates whether or not a tool modifies data or is read-only. If an
MCP server is [annotated with `readOnlyHint`](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/schema#toolannotations-readonlyhint),
it will match this filter.

types.description: A filter object to specify which tools are allowed.

types.name: McpToolRequireApproval
types.description: Specify which of the MCP server's tools require approval.

types.name: McpToolRequireApprovalMcpToolApprovalFilter
types.description:
Specify which of the MCP server's tools require approval. Can be
`always`, `never`, or a filter object associated with tools
that require approval.

types.name: McpToolRequireApprovalMcpToolApprovalSetting
types.description:
Specify a single approval policy for all tools. One of `always` or
`never`. When set to `always`, all tools will require approval. When
set to `never`, all tools will not require approval.

types.name: CodeInterpreterTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the code interpreter tool. Always `code_interpreter`.

fields.name: container
fields.description:
The code interpreter container. Can be a container ID or an object that
specifies uploaded file IDs to make available to your code, along with an
optional `memory_limit` setting.

types.description: A tool that runs Python code to help generate a response to a prompt.

types.name: CodeInterpreterToolContainer
types.description:
The code interpreter container. Can be a container ID or an object that
specifies uploaded file IDs to make available to your code, along with an
optional `memory_limit` setting.

types.name: AutoCodeInterpreterToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Always `auto`.

fields.name: file_ids
fields.description: An optional list of uploaded files to make available to your code.

fields.name: network_policy
fields.description: Network access policy for the container.

types.description: Configuration for a code interpreter container. Optionally specify the IDs of the files to run the code on.

types.name: AutoCodeInterpreterToolParamNetworkPolicy
types.description: Network access policy for the container.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Disable outbound network access. Always `disabled`.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Allow outbound network access only to specified domains. Always `allowlist`.

fields.name: allowed_domains
fields.description: A list of allowed domains when type is `allowlist`.

fields.name: domain_secrets
fields.description: Optional domain-scoped secrets for allowlisted domains.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: The domain associated with the secret.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the secret to inject for the domain.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The secret value to inject for the domain.

types.name: ImageGenTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the image generation tool. Always `image_generation`.

fields.name: quality
fields.description:
The quality of the generated image. One of `low`, `medium`, `high`,
or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

fields.name: size
fields.description: The size of the generated images. For `gpt-image-2` and `gpt-image-2-2026-04-21`, arbitrary resolutions are supported as `WIDTHxHEIGHT` strings, for example `1536x864`. Width and height must both be divisible by 16 and the requested aspect ratio must be between 1:3 and 3:1. Resolutions above `2560x1440` are experimental, and the maximum supported resolution is `3840x2160`. The requested size must also satisfy the model's current pixel and edge limits. The standard sizes `1024x1024`, `1536x1024`, and `1024x1536` are supported by the GPT image models; `auto` is supported for models that allow automatic sizing. For `dall-e-2`, use one of `256x256`, `512x512`, or `1024x1024`. For `dall-e-3`, use one of `1024x1024`, `1792x1024`, or `1024x1792`.

fields.name: output_format
fields.description:
The output format of the generated image. One of `png`, `webp`, or
`jpeg`. Default: `png`.

fields.name: output_compression
fields.description: Compression level for the output image. Default: 100.

fields.name: moderation
fields.description: Moderation level for the generated image. Default: `auto`.

fields.name: background
fields.description:
Background type for the generated image. One of `transparent`,
`opaque`, or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

fields.name: input_image_mask
fields.description:
Optional mask for inpainting. Contains `image_url`
(string, optional) and `file_id` (string, optional).

fields.name: partial_images
fields.description: Number of partial images to generate in streaming mode, from 0 (default value) to 3.

types.description: A tool that generates images using the GPT image models.

types.name: ImageGenToolModelString
types.description: The image generation model to use. Default: `gpt-image-1`.

types.name: ImageGenToolQuality
types.description:
The quality of the generated image. One of `low`, `medium`, `high`,
or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

types.name: ImageGenToolSize
types.description: The size of the generated images. For `gpt-image-2` and `gpt-image-2-2026-04-21`, arbitrary resolutions are supported as `WIDTHxHEIGHT` strings, for example `1536x864`. Width and height must both be divisible by 16 and the requested aspect ratio must be between 1:3 and 3:1. Resolutions above `2560x1440` are experimental, and the maximum supported resolution is `3840x2160`. The requested size must also satisfy the model's current pixel and edge limits. The standard sizes `1024x1024`, `1536x1024`, and `1024x1536` are supported by the GPT image models; `auto` is supported for models that allow automatic sizing. For `dall-e-2`, use one of `256x256`, `512x512`, or `1024x1024`. For `dall-e-3`, use one of `1024x1024`, `1792x1024`, or `1024x1792`.

types.name: ImageGenToolOutputFormat
types.description:
The output format of the generated image. One of `png`, `webp`, or
`jpeg`. Default: `png`.

types.name: ImageGenToolModeration
types.description: Moderation level for the generated image. Default: `auto`.

types.name: ImageGenToolBackground
types.description:
Background type for the generated image. One of `transparent`,
`opaque`, or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

types.name: InputFidelity
types.description: Control how much effort the model will exert to match the style and features, especially facial features, of input images. This parameter is only supported for `gpt-image-1` and `gpt-image-1.5` and later models, unsupported for `gpt-image-1-mini`. Supports `high` and `low`. Defaults to `low`.

types.name: ImageGenToolInputImageMask

fields.name: image_url
fields.description: Base64-encoded mask image.

fields.name: file_id
fields.description: File ID for the mask image.

types.description:
Optional mask for inpainting. Contains `image_url`
(string, optional) and `file_id` (string, optional).

types.name: LocalShellToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the local shell tool. Always `local_shell`.

types.description: A tool that allows the model to execute shell commands in a local environment.

types.name: FunctionShellToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the shell tool. Always `shell`.

types.description: A tool that allows the model to execute shell commands.

fields.name: type

types.name: CreateEvalResponsesRunDataSourceSamplingParamsText
types.description:
Configuration options for a text response from the model. Can be plain
text or structured JSON data. Learn more:
- [Text inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/text)
- [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs)

types.name: TextResponseFormatConfiguration
types.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Configuring `{ "type": "json_schema" }` enables Structured Outputs, 
which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON schema. Learn more in the 
[Structured Outputs guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

The default format is `{ "type": "text" }` with no additional options.

**Not recommended for gpt-4o and newer models:**

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.

types.name: TextResponseFormatJsonSchema

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

types.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

types.name: EvalRun

fields.name: object
fields.description: The type of the object. Always "eval.run".

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the evaluation run.

fields.name: eval_id
fields.description: The identifier of the associated evaluation.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The status of the evaluation run.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model that is evaluated, if applicable.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the evaluation run.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the evaluation run was created.

fields.name: report_url
fields.description: The URL to the rendered evaluation run report on the UI dashboard.

fields.name: result_counts
fields.description: Counters summarizing the outcomes of the evaluation run.

fields.name: per_model_usage
fields.description: Usage statistics for each model during the evaluation run.

fields.name: per_testing_criteria_results
fields.description: Results per testing criteria applied during the evaluation run.

fields.name: data_source
fields.description: Information about the run's data source.

types.description: A schema representing an evaluation run.

types.name: EvalRunResultCounts

fields.name: total
fields.description: Total number of executed output items.

fields.name: errored
fields.description: Number of output items that resulted in an error.

fields.name: failed
fields.description: Number of output items that failed to pass the evaluation.

fields.name: passed
fields.description: Number of output items that passed the evaluation.

types.description: Counters summarizing the outcomes of the evaluation run.

fields.name: model_name
fields.description: The name of the model.

fields.name: invocation_count
fields.description: The number of invocations.

fields.name: prompt_tokens
fields.description: The number of prompt tokens used.

fields.name: completion_tokens
fields.description: The number of completion tokens generated.

fields.name: total_tokens
fields.description: The total number of tokens used.

fields.name: cached_tokens
fields.description: The number of tokens retrieved from cache.

fields.name: testing_criteria
fields.description: A description of the testing criteria.

fields.name: passed
fields.description: Number of tests passed for this criteria.

fields.name: failed
fields.description: Number of tests failed for this criteria.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The error message.

types.description: An object representing an error response from the Eval API.

methods.description: Kicks off a new run for a given evaluation, specifying the data source, and what model configuration to use to test. The datasource will be validated against the schema specified in the config of the evaluation.

pathParams.name: eval_id
pathParams.description: The ID of the evaluation to create a run for.

responses.description: Successfully created a run for the evaluation
Bad request (for example, missing eval object)
